fiabilite.title: Source reliability
fiabilite.subtitle: Import your GPX field sub-sections — travel time precision improves with each session.
fiabilite.importDescription: The file is automatically split at the boundaries of the 6 monitored segments. The relative gap with the nearest Google measurement is computed for each segment.
fiabilite.evolutionDescription: One line per segment. The sky-blue dashed line at 0% is the ideal target.
fiabilite.calibrationDescription: Mean relative gap of the last {n} recordings. A positive value means Google underestimates the real time.
fiabilite.apercuDescription: Raw trace of the selected GPX, overlaid on the 6 official segments (dashed lines). Green/red markers show the boundaries of detected segments.
fiabilite.apercuPlaceholder: Select one or more GPX files to display their trace here.

segments.importDescription: Record any portion of the routes with BasicAirData GPS Logger (or any GPX app). No need to start at the official endpoint — each sub-section accumulates automatically.
segments.resumeTitle: Travel times by route — progressive precision
segments.resumeDescription: Consolidation of all field sessions. Confidence improves with each new session imported. Outbound/return mirror applied when one direction lacks data.
segments.explication1: Each GPX file covers a portion of the route between two landmarks (CARENA→GMA, DGI→Terminus 19…). No need to start at the official endpoint — any sub-section is accepted.
segments.explication2: The system sums durations of all segments from the same session to reconstruct the total crossing time per route. With multiple sessions imported, it averages them: the estimate stabilises and confidence grows progressively.
segments.explication3: Confidence combines geographic coverage (distance covered / total route distance) and the number of sessions. For 75%+, aim for 4 sessions covering 80%+ of the route.
segments.procedureTitle: How to record and improve precision
segments.comment1: Start BasicAirData GPS Logger on your phone (free, offline).
segments.comment2: Drive any portion of the routes — no need to complete the full route.
segments.comment3: Export the GPX and import it here (section above).
segments.comment4: Repeat at different times and days — precision improves automatically with each session.
segments.noteMiroirDetail: If one direction lacks data, the system temporarily uses the opposite direction's time (outbound ≈ return for the same route). Once a direct session is imported, it replaces the mirror.
segments.conseilLotDetail: Select only files belonging to the same route (e.g. all CARENA→Palm Beach segments), set its route ID and a shared session ID. Repeat for each route of the field session.

prediction.title: Crossing time by period
prediction.subtitle: Real-time data from Google Maps — comparison with field GPX times at the bottom of the page.
prediction.confrontationDesc: Times actually driven on the road — compare with Google Maps values above.

prediction.ecartPlusCourt: compared to Google Maps — Google overestimates
